S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao. amopato'oto'olrainome 'ako'oponohae akanakanae 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao. amopatoʔotoʔoɭainomə ʔakoʔoponohaə akanakanaə ʔina mənə.
S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao. amopato'oto'olrainome 'ako'oponohae akanakanae 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao. amopatoʔotoʔoɭainomə ʔakoʔoponohaə akanakanaə ʔina mənə.
S.TRANSL: 大家好。今天老師要教大家食物的魯凱語怎麼說。
Hello everyone. Today, I am going to teach you how to say the Oponoho language for food.
Do you understand what the teacher says?
老師，我不懂。可以再說一次嗎?
I don’t understand. Can you repeat it again?
我聽不到，可以大聲一點嗎?
I can’t hear you, can you speak louder?
請看這裡! 跟著我唸一遍。
Look here, please! Repeat after me.
Read it again. Please speak louder.
I forgot to bring my pen.
I forgot to bring my eraser.
請跟旁邊同學借橡皮擦。
Please borrow erasers from your classmates.
我們就上到這邊，明天再見。
Class dismissed, see you next week.
Who are you? What's your name?

S.FORM: ilrae 'a lalakeni Takanao, Kooi omiiyae alraceli.
S.PHON: iɭaə ʔa lalakəni takanao, kooi omiijaə aɭaʦəli.
S.FORM: ilrae 'a lalakeni Takanao, Kooi omiiyae alraceli.
S.PHON: iɭaə ʔa lalakəni takanao, kooi omiijaə aɭaʦəli.
S.TRANSL: 我是Takanao的兒子，我的名字叫Kooi。
I am Takanao's son, my name is Kooi.

S.FORM: ilrae 'a vanidhoni Takanao, Kooi omiiyae alraceli.
S.PHON: iɭaə ʔa vaniðoni takanao, kooi omiijaə aɭaʦəli.
S.FORM: ilrae 'a vanidhoni Takanao, Kooi omiiyae alraceli.
S.PHON: iɭaə ʔa vaniðoni takanao, kooi omiijaə aɭaʦəli.
S.TRANSL: 我是Takanao的學生，我的名字叫Kooi。
I am a student at Takanao, my name is Kacaw.
He is my brother, his name is Vaaho.
她是我的朋友，她的名字是Dheedhese。
She is my friend and her name is Dheedhese.
How many brothers and sisters do you have?

S.PHON: omikijaə takaðoʔa laataka la takaəaəa ʔaði ʔavai.
omikijaə takaðoʔa laataka la takaəaəa ʔaði ʔavai.
S.TRANSL: 我有兩個兄姐，一個妹妹。
I have two older siblings and one younger sister.
How many people are in your family?
There are four of us in our family.
How are you, Mom and Dad?
我們都平安健康，謝謝你!
We are all safe and healthy, thank you!
你的舅舅從事什麼工作?
What does your uncle do for a living?
Does your sister have a job?
Yes, I'm from the Rukai tribe.
Yes, but she's from the Paiwan tribe.
Yes, we are all very tall.
Does your sister go to school?

S.PHON: oɭihoʔokaliða ʔakoʔoponohaə ʔaðiʔo ʔaolai la ʔaðiʔo ʔavai?
oɭihoʔokaliða ʔakoʔoponohaə ʔaðiʔo ʔaolai la ʔaðiʔo ʔavai?
S.TRANSL: 你的弟弟和妹妹會說族語嗎?
Do your siblings speak the language?
Yes, they speak the ethnic language.
How many brothers and sisters do you have?
We have three brothers and sisters.
你和爸爸媽媽一起住嗎?
Do you live with your parents?
是，我和爸爸媽媽一起住。
Yes, I live with my mom and dad.

S.PHON: taʔaəɭəkaʔo omoʔo ʔaolai la omoʔo ʔavai?
taʔaəɭəkaʔo omoʔo ʔaolai la omoʔo ʔavai?
S.TRANSL: 你和爺爺奶奶一起住嗎?
Do you live with your grandparents?
不，我沒和跟他們一起住。
No, I didn't live with them.

S.PHON: taʔaəɭəɭao amali, inali la takali ʔaolai.
taʔaəɭəɭao amali, inali la takali ʔaolai.
S.TRANSL: 我跟爸爸、媽媽、哥哥住在一起。
I live with my father, mother and brother.
My hometown is in Mau Forest.
你什麼時候會回去部落?
When are you going back to the tribe?
I will go back in the summer.
The sun is coming out, it's very hot.
Is it going to be cold outside?
It's cold, so wear more clothes.

S.PHON: makəʦəɭəkai ka kikaɭapaʔa ʔina mənə, paʔapaaɭa.
makəʦəɭəkai ka kikaɭapaʔa ʔina mənə, paʔapaaɭa.
S.TRANSL: 今天不冷也不熱，很舒服。
It's neither cold nor hot today, very comfortable.

S.FORM: okaodho ka keme'e ana tevelengae, mokavolenga tahiyo.
S.PHON: okaoðo ka kəməʔə ana təvələŋaə, mokavoləŋa tahijo.
S.FORM: okaodho ka keme'e ana tevelengae, mokavolenga tahiyo.
S.PHON: okaoðo ka kəməʔə ana təvələŋaə, mokavoləŋa tahijo.
S.TRANSL: 天空沒有雲，星星出來了。
There were no clouds in the sky; the stars came out.
雨剛剛停，出了太陽，彩虹出來了。
The rain has just stopped, the sun has come out, the rainbow is coming out.
What's the weather like where you are?
We're going to have a big storm here.
Very well, out of the big sun.
I have to go to school.
The weather is very cold, so wear more clothes.

S.PHON: amoðaʔolo lo miijaŋai, ʔaiʦoʔona oməʦə soko.
amoðaʔolo lo miijaŋai, ʔaiʦoʔona oməʦə soko.
S.TRANSL: 等一下會下大雨，你要記得帶傘。
It's going to rain heavily, you have to remember to bring an umbrella.
剛剛打雷了，等一下應該就會下雨。
There was thunder just now, and it's supposed to rain later.
剛剛有下雨，現在雨停了。
It just rained, and now it's stopped.
好像下過雨了，馬路是濕的。
It seems to have rained and the road is wet.

S.FORM: odha'olo ana latadhe, oamecekali ka soko.
S.PHON: oðaʔolo ana lataðə, oaməʦəkali ka soko.
S.FORM: odha'olo ana latadhe, oamecekali ka soko.
S.PHON: oðaʔolo ana lataðə, oaməʦəkali ka soko.
S.TRANSL: 外面在下雨，我沒有帶雨傘。
It's raining outside, so I didn't bring an umbrella.

S.FORM: matoalrai tohamoho ana ve'eve'e, macalokocokiyae tave'eve'eniyae.
S.PHON: matoaɭai tohamoho ana vəʔəvəʔə, maʦalokoʦokijaə tavəʔəvəʔənijaə.
S.FORM: matoalrai tohamoho ana ve'eve'e, macalokocokiyae tave'eve'eniyae.
S.PHON: matoaɭai tohamoho ana vəʔəvəʔə, maʦalokoʦokijaə tavəʔəvəʔənijaə.
S.TRANSL: The wind is blowing so hard that my head hurts.

S.FORM: matoalrai tohamoho 'ina koli'i, malimo'iyae 'ina cokonoholi takoliya'eniyae.
S.PHON: matoaɭai tohamoho ʔina koliʔi, malimoʔijaə ʔina ʦokonoholi takolijaʔənijaə.
S.FORM: matoalrai tohamoho 'ina koli'i, malimo'iyae 'ina cokonoholi takoliya'eniyae.
S.PHON: matoaɭai tohamoho ʔina koliʔi, malimoʔijaə ʔina ʦokonoholi takolijaʔənijaə.
S.TRANSL: 太陽好大，曬得我頭好暈。
The sun is so big, it makes me dizzy.
I forgot to bring my pen.
I forgot to bring my chopsticks.
I don't have one either, let's go buy one.
Let's go buy a pen, shall we?
Well, I still have to buy erasers.
Let's go get breakfast, shall we?
No, I've already eaten at home.
I forgot to steam my lunchbox.
I forgot to bring my lunchbox.
那我們一起吃我的便當吧!
Then let's eat my lunch box together!
What are you having for lunch?
我要吃學校的營養午餐。
I want a nutritious school lunch.
What are you having for lunch?

S.PHON: oaməʦəɭao ʔaoðoɭoʔo mənə, taʔinijaəɭə konə lo ʔaanə.
oaməʦəɭao ʔaoðoɭoʔo mənə, taʔinijaəɭə konə lo ʔaanə.
S.TRANSL: 我今天有帶便當，我們中午一起吃吧!
I've brought a lunchbox today, let's eat together at noon!
Let's go play basketball, shall we?
Could you help me with the broom, please?
Okay, here's a broomstick for you.
Can you help me with the cloth?
I'm sweeping the floor, you ask Kooi to help you.
The floor is dirty, go clean it.
那個窗戶很髒，去擦一下。
That window is very dirty, go clean it.
等一下，我還沒掃完這裡。
Wait a minute, I'm not done sweeping here yet.
I'm going to see my teacher.
What do you do after school?
I'm going to sing with my friends.
She’s at home, please come in.
她不在，請問你有什麼事?
She’s not here, what can I do for you?
Grandpa, I’m ’Engelrao, please open the door.
’Ovake, this is Lee’e, I’ve come to play with you.
是啊!我很想念你。快來聊天吧!
Yeah! I miss you so much. Come on, let’s chat!
Please sit down and have some tea.

S.PHON: ʔananə ʔakosaaəni ka mataaðiʔi ʔoŋolo ʔoʦijanomi.
ʔananə ʔakosaaəni ka mataaðiʔi ʔoŋolo ʔoʦijanomi.

S.FORM: iya'e, omi'a takataadhi'i lo cengelemao 'ina ngeta madho.
S.PHON: ijaʔə, omiʔa takataaðiʔi lo ʦəŋələmao ʔina ŋəta maðo.
S.FORM: iya'e, omi'a takataadhi'i lo cengelemao 'ina ngeta madho.
S.PHON: ijaʔə, omiʔa takataaðiʔi lo ʦəŋələmao ʔina ŋəta maðo.
S.TRANSL: 好喔!這些水果看起來很棒。
媽媽煮好晚餐了，快來吃吧!
Mom has cooked dinner, come and eat!
Okay, I’ll go wash my hands.
盡量吃喝，你又不是外人。
Try to eat and drink as much as possible, you’re not an outsider.
I ate a lot, I’m full.
我要離開了，明天再來看你。
I’m leaving, I’ll see you tomorrow.

S.PHON: ijaʔə, maavaʔimao ʔolilijo ðaʔanəli lo paʔamaoɭoʔo.
ijaʔə, maavaʔimao ʔolilijo ðaʔanəli lo paʔamaoɭoʔo.
S.TRANSL: Okay, come to my house sometime.

S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao. singsi 'a amoavilimite moa valriyota 'itovolrovolro takaahodhange 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao. siŋsi ʔa amoavilimitə moa vaɭijota ʔitovoɭovoɭo takaahoðaŋə ʔina mənə.
S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao. singsi 'a amoavilimite moa valriyota 'itovolrovolro takaahodhange 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao. siŋsi ʔa amoavilimitə moa vaɭijota ʔitovoɭovoɭo takaahoðaŋə ʔina mənə.
S.TRANSL: 大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.
好喔!我們要跟耆老說萬山魯凱語。
Yes! We’re going to speak Wansan Rukai to the seniors.
Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.
你們會說萬山魯凱語啊!真是太棒了!
You guys speak Wansan Rukai! That’s great!

S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao. singsi 'a amoavilimite moa tavecengae 'adhidhapae 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao. siŋsi ʔa amoavilimitə moa tavəʦəŋaə ʔaðiðapaə ʔina mənə.
S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao. singsi 'a amoavilimite moa tavecengae 'adhidhapae 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao. siŋsi ʔa amoavilimitə moa tavəʦəŋaə ʔaðiðapaə ʔina mənə.
S.TRANSL: 大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.

S.PHON: imija moa matilivi, ʔaiʦoʔona oməʦə takijaənomi.
imija moa matilivi, ʔaiʦoʔona oməʦə takijaənomi.
S.TRANSL: 大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.
是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.
Not yet, it’s still far away.
You go get the bird catcher.
Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao, amooamita talapolapoe holaholai 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao, amooamita talapolapoə holaholai ʔina mənə.
S.FORM: aikanomi ka polringao, amooamita talapolapoe holaholai 'ina mene.
S.PHON: aikanomi ka poɭiŋao, amooamita talapolapoə holaholai ʔina mənə.
S.TRANSL: 大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.
好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
Awesome! How are we going to get there?
大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.

S.FORM: idhopele aalrame ana talicovongae, idhopele 'olra'a ana alrikodhae, lo 'initato'iidhema moa cengecengele.
S.PHON: iðopələ aaɭamə ana taliʦovoŋaə, iðopələ ʔoɭaʔa ana aɭikoðaə, lo ʔinitatoʔiiðəma moa ʦəŋəʦəŋələ.
S.FORM: idhopele aalrame ana talicovongae, idhopele 'olra'a ana alrikodhae, lo 'initato'iidhema moa cengecengele.
S.PHON: iðopələ aaɭamə ana taliʦovoŋaə, iðopələ ʔoɭaʔa ana aɭikoðaə, lo ʔinitatoʔiiðəma moa ʦəŋəʦəŋələ.
S.TRANSL: 前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.

S.FORM: makoakai, la kaimoa cengele ana aalrame.
S.PHON: makoakai, la kaimoa ʦəŋələ ana aaɭamə.
S.FORM: makoakai, la kaimoa cengele ana aalrame.
S.PHON: makoakai, la kaimoa ʦəŋələ ana aaɭamə.
S.TRANSL: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

S.PHON: amoikimita ʔina ʔasahovo lo moaŋai takaəaəa zikaŋə.
amoikimita ʔina ʔasahovo lo moaŋai takaəaəa zikaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 我們一個小時後回來集合。
We’ll be back in an hour.

S.PHON: oʦəŋələɭao takapahanaanaə ʔoɭaʔa, laʔi kapahanaanaə aaɭamə.
oʦəŋələɭao takapahanaanaə ʔoɭaʔa, laʔi kapahanaanaə aaɭamə.
S.TRANSL: 我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.
Hurry up, we have to go.
Wait for me, my feet are tired.
There are a lot of people swimming here.
那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
What kind of car did you take?
How do you go to church?

S.PHON: ʔoŋtiŋi saŋəpahə amali ovilijaə moa kijokai.
ʔoŋtiŋi saŋəpahə amali ovilijaə moa kijokai.

S.TRANSL: How does he usually go to school?
He took the subway to school.

S.PHON: apokoani lo moani loŋai ʔaðamai omoʔo ʔavai?
apokoani lo moani loŋai ʔaðamai omoʔo ʔavai?
S.TRANSL: How does your grandmother go to the grocery store?
She went to the grocery store on a motorcycle.
How are you going to get to Orchid Island?
I want to take a boat to Lanyu.
How did he get to Orchid Island?
He took a plane to Orchid Island.
What bus did he take to Kaohsiung?
He took the THSR to Kaohsiung.
Did he take a train to Taitung?
Yes, he took a train to Taitung.
Did he take a train to Taitung?
No, he took a plane to Taitung.
Is there a bus stop near here?

S.FORM: omiki, dhoacaa 'iidheme talicovongae amocengelemo'o ta'adha'adhaoe sangepahe.
S.PHON: omiki, ðoaʦaa ʔiiðəmə taliʦovoŋaə amoʦəŋələmoʔo taʔaðaʔaðaoə saŋəpahə.
S.FORM: omiki, dhoacaa 'iidheme talicovongae amocengelemo'o ta'adha'adhaoe sangepahe.
S.PHON: omiki, ðoaʦaa ʔiiðəmə taliʦovoŋaə amoʦəŋələmoʔo taʔaðaʔaðaoə saŋəpahə.
S.TRANSL: 有，往前走就會看到公車站了。
Yes, if you go straight ahead, you will see the bus stop.
離這裡最近的捷運站在哪裡?
Where is the nearest MRT station?

S.FORM: okaodho 'ina tai'adha'adhae ciingi, kasani haiya dholroa'o 'anopapalra.
S.PHON: okaoðo ʔina taiʔaðaʔaðaə ʦiiŋi, kasani haija ðoɭoaʔo ʔanopapaɭa.
S.FORM: okaodho 'ina tai'adha'adhae ciingi, kasani haiya dholroa'o 'anopapalra.
S.PHON: okaoðo ʔina taiʔaðaʔaðaə ʦiiŋi, kasani haija ðoɭoaʔo ʔanopapaɭa.
S.TRANSL: 這裡沒有捷運站，你只能坐計程車。
There is no MRT station here, you can only take a taxi.
How long does it take to get to the school by bus?

S.FORM: mamangele la kalrima pongkange ila okela gako.
S.PHON: mamaŋələ la kaɭima poŋkaŋə ila okəla gako.
S.FORM: mamangele la kalrima pongkange ila okela gako.
S.PHON: mamaŋələ la kaɭima poŋkaŋə ila okəla gako.

S.TRANSL: 搭高鐵要多久時間會到臺中?
How long does it take to get to Taichung by THSR?
I will come to Taichung in an hour.
Is it a long drive to Ulai?
你搭捷運來我家要多久?
How long did it take you to come to my house by subway?
Do you know how to ride a bicycle?
I have something to ask you?
I have something to ask you?
Don't, I don't know much about it.

S.PHON: paʔamaoɭokaʔo lo ʔaanəŋa? ðoɭokalimijaʔə moa ʦəŋələ?
paʔamaoɭokaʔo lo ʔaanəŋa? ðoɭokalimijaʔə moa ʦəŋələ?
S.TRANSL: 你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?
Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

S.PHON: paʔamaoɭokaʔo loʔiða? naoʔitovoɭimijaə paohiki ʔina lamolai ʔoponoho?
paʔamaoɭokaʔo loʔiða? naoʔitovoɭimijaə paohiki ʔina lamolai ʔoponoho?
S.TRANSL: 你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於萬山的傳說故事。
Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary story of Wanshan.

S.FORM: amooalrao lrehelrehe lo'idha, amoikikali ka dha'ane.
S.PHON: amooaɭao ɭəhəɭəhə loʔiða, amoikikali ka ðaʔanə.
S.FORM: amooalrao lrehelrehe lo'idha, amoikikali ka dha'ane.
S.PHON: amooaɭao ɭəhəɭəhə loʔiða, amoikikali ka ðaʔanə.
S.TRANSL: 明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

S.FORM: osiyalalralrao olriho'o omiimiya'e 'ina dhekai tapapiya'edha 'aamadhalae, nao'itovolrine omiyalraimiya'e.
S.PHON: osijalaɭaɭao oɭihoʔo omiimijaʔə ʔina ðəkai tapapijaʔəða ʔaamaðalaə, naoʔitovoɭinə omijaɭaimijaʔə.
S.FORM: osiyalalralrao olriho'o omiimiya'e 'ina dhekai tapapiya'edha 'aamadhalae, nao'itovolrine omiyalraimiya'e.
S.PHON: osijalaɭaɭao oɭihoʔo omiimijaʔə ʔina ðəkai tapapijaʔəða ʔaamaðalaə, naoʔitovoɭinə omijaɭaimijaʔə.
S.TRANSL: 我聽說您對魯凱族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Rukai people.

S.FORM: madhaokai ka alriho'aeli, oha'eve la alralraimiya'e pato'o ona talriho'aeli.

Is your wife also a Rukai?
No, she's from the Paiwan tribe.
我會說萬山魯凱語和多納魯凱語。
I speak Wansan Rukai and Donal Rukai.

S.FORM: mahavehavehelrao tohamoho ta'itovolroelimiya'e 'ina mene, loiyae pato'o dhona, kaanaedha pelehe'edha lo moadha olopo?

S.FORM: iya'e, omoamoalrao lrehelrehe olopo ona akasavaheli.

S.TRANSL: 好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

S.FORM: icongoonga'o！ tohamoho ka mataadhi'i tapato'oto'oe'iyae 'ina mene.
S.PHON: iʦoŋooŋaʔo* tohamoho ka mataaðiʔi tapatoʔotoʔoəʔijaə ʔina mənə.
S.FORM: icongoonga'o！ tohamoho ka mataadhi'i tapato'oto'oe'iyae 'ina mene.

S.TRANSL: 謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.
我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

S.PHON: pataʔasamita ʔina taitovoɭaəlimijaʔə ʔina mənə. iʦoŋooŋaʔo tapatoʔijaə

S.PHON: amoðaaʦəŋaɭao, iðamaɭao amokinakaðaoə lamolai lo ʔoəlaaŋata.

I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.
Hello, what do you want to buy?
I want to buy a cup.
Hello, what do you want to buy?
I want to buy a hat.
我要買杯子，你們有賣嗎?

Yes, how many do you want to buy?
No, we don't sell betel nut.

All the sweet potatoes are sold out.

We only have one bottle left.
How many pounds do you want to buy?
How many pounds of eggs do you want to buy?
I want to buy two catties.
I want to buy two green onions.
There are three left, all for you!
How much is this bag of rice?

不行，這個已經很便宜了。
No, this is already very cheap.
I want this packet of cookies.

$30 for the cookies, anything else you need?
Can I borrow money from you?
Yes! How much do you need?

S.PHON: mahimohoɭao oməʦə paiso, ðoɭokalimijaʔə ʔipasoɭija paiso?
mahimohoɭao oməʦə paiso, ðoɭokalimijaʔə ʔipasoɭija paiso?
S.TRANSL: 我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?
I don't have any money either!

S.PHON: mahimohoɭao oməʦə paiso, ðoɭokalimijaʔə ʔipasoɭija paiso?
mahimohoɭao oməʦə paiso, ðoɭokalimijaʔə ʔipasoɭija paiso?
S.TRANSL: 我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

No, you haven't paid me back the money you borrowed last time.
請借給我你的腳踏車好嗎?
Can I borrow your bicycle, please?
Okay, but you have to give it back to me tomorrow.

It's raining, I don't have an umbrella, can you lend me one?
我只有一把傘，我們一起撐吧!
I only have an umbrella, let's stick together!

S.PHON: maamaikai ʔina pasoɭijaəʔijaə solatə ʔamaðalaə, ʔaiɭiɭaimijaʔə.

S.PHON: lo naoʦəŋələŋa solatə mijaʔo ʔa patoʔoijaə.

When do I have to pay you back?
沒關係，你有錢的時候再說。
It's okay, you can talk about it when you have money.
When are you going to pay me back?
I'll return it to you tomorrow.
When are you going to return my book?
我昨天不是已經還你了嗎?
Didn't I return it to you yesterday?
Did Ohosane lend you this pen?
Yes, I borrowed it from him today.
Did you borrow this pen from Ohosane?
No, this is what mom bought.

S.FORM: okaodhonga timo, moine Mooni 'ipasolriya tosii'i timo.

Go tell Mooni to give me my money back.
How much does she owe you?
I’m going to plant vegetables in the garden.

S.PHON: maðao tohamoho ʔoɭao, osivatəɭao, ʦəvana amopoaʦijaʦilaiɭao.

There's too much grass, I'm cutting it, I'll have to water it later.

S.PHON: omaɭamaijaə takiɭoŋo la kaolo poa ʔina.
omaɭamaijaə takiɭoŋo la kaolo poa ʔina.
S.TRANSL: 幫我把鐮刀和鋤頭拿來好嗎?
Will you get my scythes and hoes, please?
Did you turn over the soil?
我剛翻過土，等等要播種。
I just turned over the soil, and so on to sow seeds.
你種的菜可以收成了嗎?
Are your vegetables ready to be harvested?

The cabbage here is ready to be harvested, but the Showa grass there is not yet ready to be harvested.
哇!你挖了這麼多的地瓜。
(sighs) Wow! You dug up so many gourds.
Will you help me with the barn?
We're releasing fishing nets to catch fish.

S.PHON: maðaoŋa aɭanai kaʔaŋə ʔina taiki vakahə.
maðaoŋa aɭanai kaʔaŋə ʔina taiki vakahə.

The fish basket has been full of fish.
How do you usually catch fish?
I shoot fish with a spear.
Do you know how to hunt with a bow and arrow?

S.FORM: oahadhopelelrao tokoho lo malrali alopae, omiki lo 'ikoangeli 'ita'olroli olopo.

I usually catch my prey with a trap, but occasionally I hunt with a gun and a dog.
This is the road the boar took.

S.PHON: ðapaləni ʔina vavoi, ʔatolohoɭao ɭihoʔo ʔoloni vavoi.

S.TRANSL: 這是山豬的腳印，所以這是山豬所走的路。
These are the footprints of the boar, so this is the path the boar took.

S.FORM: moa 'inikookono cengele tokohota, maelre taoa motokoho.

Go and see our trap, for it seems that we have caught our prey.
狗好像聞到味道了。我們快跟過去。
The dog seems to have smelled it. Let's follow them.
你正在製作的是什麼東西?
這是石壓式陷阱，用來抓老鼠的。
This is a stone trap, used to catch rats.
你放在那裡的是什麼東西?
What is that thing you put there?

S.PHON: ʔahaki patokəɭəʦə ana, toʔahakinai poɭa akəʦə.
ʔahaki patokəɭəʦə ana, toʔahakinai poɭa akəʦə.
S.TRANSL: 那是套腳式繩索陷阱，我們用它來抓山羌。
It was a leg-rigged rope trap, and we used it to catch Shankiang.

S.FORM: icongoongai tapatahomaniname loomo, madhao alranai alopae 'ina mene.

Thanks to the blessing of our ancestors, we had a great harvest today.
是啊!我們帶回部落好好分享吧!
Yeah! Let's take it back to the tribe and share it!

Hello everyone, my name is Takanao.
Hello everyone, my name is Hipono.

ʔaðili ʔavai aɭaʦəða ʔa saiðai omiiðə.

How old is your elder brother?
How old is your younger brother?
Do you have schools in your community?
你在部落的學校讀書嗎？
Do you study in your tribe’s school?

S.FORM: iya'e, mikinai gako solasolate omi'anai dha'ananae.

Yes, we are like family while we are studying at school.
你的媽媽從事什麼工作？
你的爸爸從事什麼工作？
不是， 他是我弟弟的朋友。
No, he is my younger brother’s friend.
不是， 她是我的妹妹。
No, she is my younger sister.
How many people in their family?

How many children in your family?

How many younger siblings do you have?

S.PHON: omiki lapoli ta kaəaəa taʔoɭo la ta kaəaəa aaɭamə, ðaʔananaaəli analo.
S.TRANSL: 我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

S.FORM: olapoolrao ta kaeaea ngiao, lo mikini alapoemao ta'aelre ka masahengehekamao.
S.PHON: olapooɭao ta kaəaəa ŋiao, lo mikini alapoəmao taʔaəɭə ka masahəŋəhəkamao.
S.FORM: olapoolrao ta kaeaea ngiao, lo mikini alapoemao ta'aelre ka masahengehekamao.
S.PHON: olapooɭao ta kaəaəa ŋiao, lo mikini alapoəmao taʔaəɭə ka masahəŋəhəkamao.
S.TRANSL: 我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

S.PHON: nao ʔitovoɭimijaʔə pataliki pələhəaʔəni ta alopo.
nao ʔitovoɭimijaʔə pataliki pələhəaʔəni ta alopo.
S.TRANSL: 我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

S.FORM: todhaokali ka lriho'o, alrihoa'eli amo poeelrelraimiya'e pato'o.
S.PHON: toðaokali ka ɭihoʔo, aɭihoaʔəli amo poəəɭəɭaimijaʔə patoʔo.
S.FORM: todhaokali ka lriho'o, alrihoa'eli amo poeelrelraimiya'e pato'o.
S.PHON: toðaokali ka ɭihoʔo, aɭihoaʔəli amo poəəɭəɭaimijaʔə patoʔo.
S.TRANSL: 我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.
你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

S.PHON: ʔahakikinai olopo oðoɭokai ka poɭa aʔivivai.
ʔahakikinai olopo oðoɭokai ka poɭa aʔivivai.
S.TRANSL: 我們打獵的武器不可以讓女生碰到。
Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.
那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
Then, can women go hunting together?
不行，女人也不可以打獵。
No, women cannot go hunting either.

S.FORM: Vaaho, dha'ane ikaodhonga miciyo, moa longai miciyo.
S.PHON: vaaho, ðaʔanə ikaoðoŋa miʦijo, moa loŋai miʦijo.
S.FORM: Vaaho, dha'ane ikaodhonga miciyo, moa longai miciyo.
S.PHON: vaaho, ðaʔanə ikaoðoŋa miʦijo, moa loŋai miʦijo.
S.TRANSL: Vaaho，家裡沒有米酒了，你去買米酒。
Vaaho, our home is out of rice wine, please go and buy some.

S.FORM: tahaha'ae omikina ta kaeaea paniya, alangaalraenga'o!
S.PHON: tahahaʔaə omikina ta kaəaəa panija, alaŋaaɭaəŋaʔo!
S.FORM: tahaha'ae omikina ta kaeaea paniya, alangaalraenga'o!
S.PHON: tahahaʔaə omikina ta kaəaəa panija, alaŋaaɭaəŋaʔo!
S.TRANSL: 廚房裡還有一瓶，不用買吧!
There is still one bottle left in the kitchen, so there is no need to buy one!

S.FORM: ta kaeaea paniya ka molekatekai, moana longai matahadho'a paniya.
S.PHON: ta kaəaəa panija ka moləkatəkai, moana loŋai matahaðoʔa panija.
S.FORM: ta kaeaea paniya ka molekatekai, moana longai matahadho'a paniya.
S.PHON: ta kaəaəa panija ka moləkatəkai, moana loŋai matahaðoʔa panija.
S.TRANSL: 一瓶不夠，再去買兩瓶回來。
One bottle is not enough for me, go and buy two more back.
我要買米酒，你們有賣嗎?
I am looking for rice wine. Do you have any rice wine?
Yes! How many bottles do you need?

S.FORM: amo langailrao ta kadho'a paniya, lamo laangailrao ta kaeaea tavisie pahai.
S.PHON: amo laŋaiɭao ta kaðoʔa panija, lamo laaŋaiɭao ta kaəaəa tavisiə pahai.
S.FORM: amo langailrao ta kadho'a paniya, lamo laangailrao ta kaeaea tavisie pahai.
S.PHON: amo laŋaiɭao ta kaðoʔa panija, lamo laaŋaiɭao ta kaəaəa tavisiə pahai.
S.TRANSL: 我要買兩瓶，還要買一包米。
I want two bottles and a pack of rice.

S.FORM: omiki 'ina pahai, omikinakai nao alra aiyae'o?
S.PHON: omiki ʔina pahai, omikinakai nao aɭa aijaəʔo?
S.FORM: omiki 'ina pahai, omikinakai nao alra aiyae'o?
S.PHON: omiki ʔina pahai, omikinakai nao aɭa aijaəʔo?
S.TRANSL: 米在這裡，你還需要什麼嗎?
Here is the rice, what else do you need?
I want some eggs too, do you have some?
I want to buy some clothes.
What kind of clothes do you like?
I want to buy the clothes with short sleeves.
We have white and blue ones too.
請給我一件藍色的衣服。
Please give me the clothes in blue.

S.PHON: vaaho ʔinainana aoaəʔo? omoakaʔo ʔoəʔəʔəhə ʦoɭoʦoɭoʔo?
vaaho ʔinainana aoaəʔo? omoakaʔo ʔoəʔəʔəhə ʦoɭoʦoɭoʔo?
S.TRANSL: vaaho你剛剛去哪裡了?你去運動嗎?
vaaho, where have you been just now? Did you go exercising?

S.PHON: ʔaina, omoaɭao pasopaɭa omoli ʔavai loŋai aaʔo.
ʔaina, omoaɭao pasopaɭa omoli ʔavai loŋai aaʔo.
S.TRANSL: No, I went grocery shopping for my grandma.

S.FORM: kapi'a'o ana, pa'amaolroka'o lo moongo? aiki'iyae kamo 'iniyaelree kone.
S.PHON: kapiʔaʔo ana, paʔamaoɭokaʔo lo mooŋo? aikiʔijaə kamo ʔinijaəɭəə konə.
S.FORM: kapi'a'o ana, pa'amaolroka'o lo moongo? aiki'iyae kamo 'iniyaelree kone.
S.PHON: kapiʔaʔo ana, paʔamaoɭokaʔo lo mooŋo? aikiʔijaə kamo ʔinijaəɭəə konə.
S.TRANSL: 這樣啊，你晚上有沒有空?要不要和我一起吃飯。
No wonder! Are you available at night? Would you like to have a meal with me?
No, I want to buy some clothes.
Then, I will go with you.

S.PHON: nao ʔitovoɭimijaʔə mapini paiso ʔina aaʔo?
nao ʔitovoɭimijaʔə mapini paiso ʔina aaʔo?
S.TRANSL: 你好，請問這把菜多少錢?
Hello, how much is this bunch of vegetables?
The vegetables are $20 for a bunch.

S.FORM: 'ina maihange mapini ta kaeaea tingatingi paiso?
S.PHON: ʔina maihaŋə mapini ta kaəaəa tiŋatiŋi paiso?
S.FORM: 'ina maihange mapini ta kaeaea tingatingi paiso?
S.PHON: ʔina maihaŋə mapini ta kaəaəa tiŋatiŋi paiso?
S.TRANSL: How much are these sweet potatoes?

S.FORM: maihange ta kaeaea tingatingi mamatolrolo paiso.
S.PHON: maihaŋə ta kaəaəa tiŋatiŋi mamatoɭolo paiso.
S.FORM: maihange ta kaeaea tingatingi mamatolrolo paiso.
S.PHON: maihaŋə ta kaəaəa tiŋatiŋi mamatoɭolo paiso.
S.TRANSL: Sweet potatoes are thirty dollars for a pound.

S.FORM: vo'alriyae ta kaeaea 'evehe aa'o la ta kaeaea tingatingi maihange.
S.PHON: voʔaɭijaə ta kaəaəa ʔəvəhə aaʔo la ta kaəaəa tiŋatiŋi maihaŋə.
S.FORM: vo'alriyae ta kaeaea 'evehe aa'o la ta kaeaea tingatingi maihange.
S.PHON: voʔaɭijaə ta kaəaəa ʔəvəhə aaʔo la ta kaəaəa tiŋatiŋi maihaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 請給我兩把菜和一斤地瓜。
Please give me two bunches of vegetables and a pound of sweet potatoes.

S.FORM: vaaho, mahimoholrao omece ingpice, amo dholro ka'iyae pasolria?
S.PHON: vaaho, mahimohoɭao oməʦə iŋpiʦə, amo ðoɭo kaʔijaə pasoɭia?
S.FORM: vaaho, mahimoholrao omece ingpice, amo dholro ka'iyae pasolria?
S.PHON: vaaho, mahimohoɭao oməʦə iŋpiʦə, amo ðoɭo kaʔijaə pasoɭia?
S.TRANSL: vaaho，我忘了帶筆，你可以借我嗎?
vaaho, I forgot to bring my pen. Can you lend me one?
I don’t have one either. This is dhipolo’s.
那可以借我一下子嗎?我很快就還你。
Can you lend me the pen for a second? I will return to you immediately.
Thank you and thank dhipolo for me.
I want to return these two books.
No problem and what else do you need?

S.PHON: nao ʔipasoɭijana ta katoɭo solatə omijaɭao.
nao ʔipasoɭijana ta katoɭo solatə omijaɭao.
S.TRANSL: I would like to borrow these three books.

S.PHON: ijaʔə. la ʔaiʦoʔona lo ʔapakatoɭolo mənanaə oʔaiɭimao.
ijaʔə. la ʔaiʦoʔona lo ʔapakatoɭolo mənanaə oʔaiɭimao.
S.TRANSL: 好了。記得三天後要拿來還。
Sure and remember to return them after three days.
You haven’t returned me the eraser.
那我的橡皮擦怎麼不見了。
Then, why is my eraser missing?
Did you lend it to vaaho?
I don’t know. I will go and ask her.
I will lend you my eraser first.
dhipolo, did vaaho lend you this coat?
不是，這是我媽媽買給我的。
No, my mom bought it for me.
vaaho’s coat is the same as yours.

S.PHON: ʔapokavoləvoləða ʔa taəʦəɭaŋə, mahamaokai ka tapaʦaʔaəninə.
ʔapokavoləvoləða ʔa taəʦəɭaŋə, mahamaokai ka tapaʦaʔaəninə.
S.TRANSL: 他的外套是黑色的，我們的顏色不一樣。
His coat is black, so our colors are different.
dhipolo, did you borrow money from vaaho?

S.FORM: iya'e, nao 'ailridhe omiyalraidhe men, la kahimoholrao omece paiso.
S.PHON: ijaʔə, nao ʔaiɭiðə omijaɭaiðə mən, la kahimohoɭao oməʦə paiso.
S.FORM: iya'e, nao 'ailridhe omiyalraidhe men, la kahimoholrao omece paiso.
S.PHON: ijaʔə, nao ʔaiɭiðə omijaɭaiðə mən, la kahimohoɭao oməʦə paiso.
S.TRANSL: 有，我本來今天要還他，我忘記帶錢了。
Yes, I was supposed to return to him today, but I forgot to bring the money.
Then, when will you return the money?
I will return to him tomorrow.
Okay, I will tell vaaho for you.

S.FORM: a'ata'atange toha'akine apoto totangetange polra, tikohai to'ahakine kelrece polra, nitopodhaolae.
S.PHON: aʔataʔataŋə tohaʔakinə apoto totaŋətaŋə poɭa, tikohai toʔahakinə kəɭəʦə poɭa, nitopoðaolaə.
S.FORM: a'ata'atange toha'akine apoto totangetange polra, tikohai to'ahakine kelrece polra, nitopodhaolae.
S.PHON: aʔataʔataŋə tohaʔakinə apoto totaŋətaŋə poɭa, tikohai toʔahakinə kəɭəʦə poɭa, nitopoðaolaə.
S.TRANSL: 老鼠應該要⽤⽯壓式陷阱抓，⽵雞應該要⽤套頸式陷阱抓，這樣才對。
It is correct to catch mice through rangay and catch bamboo partridges through qjiulang.

S.FORM: kapi'anineana, paolilrao tapapi'ae! alakali kilaaka poolra.
S.PHON: kapiʔaninəana, paoliɭao tapapiʔaə! alakali kilaaka pooɭa.
S.FORM: kapi'anineana, paolilrao tapapi'ae! alakali kilaaka poolra.
S.PHON: kapiʔaninəana, paoliɭao tapapiʔaə! alakali kilaaka pooɭa.
S.TRANSL: 原來是這樣，我都搞錯了！難怪我都抓不到。
No wonder! I am mistaken and that’s why I cannot catch any of them.

S.FORM: la nao 'aelraangimiya'e pato'o ta kadho'a tapapi'ae tokoho.
S.PHON: la nao ʔaəɭaaŋimijaʔə patoʔo ta kaðoʔa tapapiʔaə tokoho.
S.FORM: la nao 'aelraangimiya'e pato'o ta kadho'a tapapi'ae tokoho.
S.PHON: la nao ʔaəɭaaŋimijaʔə patoʔo ta kaðoʔa tapapiʔaə tokoho.
S.TRANSL: 那我再教你怎麼製作這兩種陷阱。
Then I will teach you how to set up these two traps.

S.FORM: iya'e! 'inkoolarli! pi'ali ana lamo dholrongalrao malra alopae lo vilrilaenga.
S.PHON: ijaʔə! ʔinkoolarli! piʔali ana lamo ðoɭoŋaɭao maɭa alopaə lo viɭilaəŋa.
S.FORM: iya'e! 'inkoolarli! pi'ali ana lamo dholrongalrao malra alopae lo vilrilaenga.
S.PHON: ijaʔə! ʔinkoolarli! piʔali ana lamo ðoɭoŋaɭao maɭa alopaə lo viɭilaəŋa.
S.TRANSL: 好啊！太棒了！這樣我以後就會抓到獵物了。
Great! I will be able to catch the prey in the future.

S.FORM: ooma 'olrao 'oasangedha'a, moa ta'ekelae malra takilrongo, taoa ooma.
S.PHON: ooma ʔoɭao ʔoasaŋəðaʔa, moa taʔəkəlaə maɭa takiɭoŋo, taoa ooma.
S.FORM: ooma 'olrao 'oasangedha'a, moa ta'ekelae malra takilrongo, taoa ooma.
S.PHON: ooma ʔoɭao ʔoasaŋəðaʔa, moa taʔəkəlaə maɭa takiɭoŋo, taoa ooma.
S.TRANSL: 田裡的草太長了，你去倉庫拿鐮刀，我們去田裡。
The grass in the fields is too tall. Go to the warehouse to take the sickle and let’s go to the fields.

S.FORM: omiki vakahe vila'a, mo'adhinga ta'ekelae lamo cengelemo'o.
S.PHON: omiki vakahə vilaʔa, moʔaðiŋa taʔəkəlaə lamo ʦəŋələmoʔo.
S.FORM: omiki vakahe vila'a, mo'adhinga ta'ekelae lamo cengelemo'o.
S.PHON: omiki vakahə vilaʔa, moʔaðiŋa taʔəkəlaə lamo ʦəŋələmoʔo.
S.TRANSL: 在簍子的旁邊，你進去穀倉就會看到了。
It is next to the round basket. You will see it when you enter the warehouse.
小心拿，不要受傷。鐮刀很利。
Be careful and don’t get hurt. The sickle is very sharp.
我的陷阱在那棵樹的下面。
My trap is under that tree.

S.PHON: totaɭihoʔaəɭao piki ʔina, oɭihoʔoɭao taikiəða tokoholi.
totaɭihoʔaəɭao piki ʔina, oɭihoʔoɭao taikiəða tokoholi.
S.TRANSL: 我在這邊有做記號，我知道我的陷阱在哪裡。
I make a mark here, so I know where my trap is.

S.PHON: ʔina ʔa ʔatokaʔakaʔaŋə ɭoŋolo vakahə, opoanai piki ɭana ʔikaʔakaʔaŋə.
ʔina ʔa ʔatokaʔakaʔaŋə ɭoŋolo vakahə, opoanai piki ɭana ʔikaʔakaʔaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 這是魚筌，我們放在溪邊捕魚。
This is a fish trap which we put beside the river for fishing.

S.FORM: tikinai ana ikaodhiname 'atoka'aka'ange lrongolo vakahe, oahatoha'akinai lakisi lroikikisi.
S.PHON: tikinai ana ikaoðinamə ʔatokaʔakaʔaŋə ɭoŋolo vakahə, oahatohaʔakinai lakisi ɭoikikisi.
S.FORM: tikinai ana ikaodhiname 'atoka'aka'ange lrongolo vakahe, oahatoha'akinai lakisi lroikikisi.
S.PHON: tikinai ana ikaoðinamə ʔatokaʔakaʔaŋə ɭoŋolo vakahə, oahatohaʔakinai lakisi ɭoikikisi.
S.TRANSL: 我們那邊沒有魚筌，我們都用釣竿抓魚。
We don’t have any fish traps there, so we catch fish with a fishing rod.

S.PHON: toʔahakinakali ka ɭakisi, ʔoəlaŋata amo patoʔotoʔomʔijaə?
toʔahakinakali ka ɭakisi, ʔoəlaŋata amo patoʔotoʔomʔijaə?
S.TRANSL: 我沒有用過釣竿，你下次教我好嗎?
I have never used a fishing rod before, would you mind teaching me next time?

S.PHON: ijaʔə, ʔoəlaŋata ʔanoaəɭə mita moa ɭoikikisi.
ijaʔə, ʔoəlaŋata ʔanoaəɭə mita moa ɭoikikisi.
S.TRANSL: 好啊!我們下次一起去釣魚。
Sure! Let’s go fishing together next time.

S.PHON: lo ʔoəlaŋata amo patoʔotoʔoɭaimijaʔə ʔatokaʔakaʔaŋə ɭoŋolo vakahə.
lo ʔoəlaŋata amo patoʔotoʔoɭaimijaʔə ʔatokaʔakaʔaŋə ɭoŋolo vakahə.
S.TRANSL: I will teach you how to make a fish trap next time.

S.PHON: ɭakisiʔo ʔaələləŋa, kioɭakisiŋai kaʔaŋə! pakonoa vilivili.
ɭakisiʔo ʔaələləŋa, kioɭakisiŋai kaʔaŋə! pakonoa vilivili.
S.TRANSL: 你的釣竿動了，是不是有魚上鉤了!你快拉起來。
Your fishing rod is moving. Isn’t the fish on the hook? Pull it up!

S.FORM: cevanana, 'aelele tohamoho apange. nao iki ina vilivili lrakisi, moa anaheea pasopalriyae malra 'asiyo.
S.PHON: ʦəvanana, ʔaələlə tohamoho apaŋə. nao iki ina vilivili ɭakisi, moa anahəəa pasopaɭijaə maɭa ʔasijo.
S.FORM: cevanana, 'aelele tohamoho apange. nao iki ina vilivili lrakisi, moa anaheea pasopalriyae malra 'asiyo.
S.PHON: ʦəvanana, ʔaələlə tohamoho apaŋə. nao iki ina vilivili ɭakisi, moa anahəəa pasopaɭijaə maɭa ʔasijo.
S.TRANSL: 等等，船太晃了。我在這邊拉釣竿，你去那邊幫我拿網子來。
Wait, the ship is rolling heavily. I am here to pull the fishing rod and you should go there to grab me the net.

S.FORM: poiyae vilivili ka'ange 'ina. nao pasopalrimiya'e vilivili.
S.PHON: poijaə vilivili kaʔaŋə ʔina. nao pasopaɭimijaʔə vilivili.
S.FORM: poiyae vilivili ka'ange 'ina. nao pasopalrimiya'e vilivili.
S.PHON: poijaə vilivili kaʔaŋə ʔina. nao pasopaɭimijaʔə vilivili.
S.TRANSL: 你把魚拉到我這邊。我幫妳拉起來。
Pull the fish to my side and I will help you pull it up.

S.FORM: 'akosaaeni tadhi'ae! madhao alrata mene, so'etenga vakahe ka'ange.
S.PHON: ʔakosaaəni taðiʔaə! maðao aɭata mənə, soʔətəŋa vakahə kaʔaŋə.
S.FORM: 'akosaaeni tadhi'ae! madhao alrata mene, so'etenga vakahe ka'ange.
S.PHON: ʔakosaaəni taðiʔaə! maðao aɭata mənə, soʔətəŋa vakahə kaʔaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 太好了!今天大豐收，魚簍都裝滿了。
Great! It is a bumper crop today with full fish baskets.
我們快回去和大家分享。
Let’s go back immediately and share it with everyone.
不要再玩了，快坐在座位上。
Stop playing and quickly sit in your seat.

S.FORM: mene 'a kingyoobi, lo 'idhanga amo ivalriyo, ta 'anoaelre moa 'ooliliyo!
S.PHON: mənə ʔa kiŋjoobi, lo ʔiðaŋa amo ivaɭijo, ta ʔanoaəɭə moa ʔoolilijo!
S.FORM: mene 'a kingyoobi, lo 'idhanga amo ivalriyo, ta 'anoaelre moa 'ooliliyo!
S.PHON: mənə ʔa kiŋjoobi, lo ʔiðaŋa amo ivaɭijo, ta ʔanoaəɭə moa ʔoolilijo!
S.TRANSL: 今天星期五，明天放假，一起出去玩吧!
It’s Friday today, we have a day off tomorrow. Let’s go out and play together!
Great! Let’s go to the riverside.
What day will it be tomorrow?

S.PHON: lo ʔiðaŋa ʔa suijoobi, ʔaanə ta oa ʔosahijo maɭi!
lo ʔiðaŋa ʔa suijoobi, ʔaanə ta oa ʔosahijo maɭi!
S.TRANSL: 明天星期三，我們明天下午去玩球吧!
Tomorrow will be Wednesday. Let’s go play ball tomorrow afternoon!
What will you be doing tomorrow morning?

S.FORM: neiciyoobi lo 'idha, amo owanai kiwkai inali.
S.PHON: nəiʦijoobi lo ʔiða, amo owanai kiwkai inali.
S.FORM: neiciyoobi lo 'idha, amo owanai kiwkai inali.

S.TRANSL: 明天是星期日，我要和媽媽去教會。
Tomorrow will be Sunday, I will be going to church with my mom.
I feel like vomiting. I need to go to the restroom.

S.FORM: sinsi, o'ange'angiyae vahangeli, nao owana tave'ekae.
S.PHON: sinsi, oʔaŋəʔaŋijaə vahaŋəli, nao owana tavəʔəkaə.

S.TRANSL: 老師，我肚子痛，要去廁所。
Teacher, I have a stomachache. I need to go to the restroom.
May I go wash my hands?

S.FORM: dhalinga i'adha ta solate, cevanana lamo owa mo'o.
S.PHON: ðaliŋa iʔaða ta solatə, ʦəvanana lamo owa moʔo.

S.TRANSL: It’s almost time for class to end. Wait a little longer.

S.PHON: sinsi, maʦalokoʦokijaə, lo ʔaanə amo ivaɭijoɭao.
sinsi, maʦalokoʦokijaə, lo ʔaanə amo ivaɭijoɭao.
S.TRANSL: 老師，我頭好痛，下午要請假。
It’s almost time for class to end. Wait a little longer.
Okay, take care and go home.

S.FORM: sinsi, lo giceyoobi amo ivalriyolrao, amo owalrao 'icengele koisange.

S.TRANSL: 老師，我下禮拜一要請假，我要去看醫生。
Teacher, I need to take a sick leave next Monday. I have to see a doctor.
She has a stomachache. She’s in the restroom.

S.FORM: sinsi, Vaaho o'ange'angidhe colo'olraedha, ivalriyo mene.
S.PHON: sinsi, vaaho oʔaŋəʔaŋiðə ʦoloʔoɭaəða, ivaɭijo mənə.

S.TRANSL: Teacher, Vaaho has a sore throat. She took a sick leave today.

S.PHON: aɭaʦəli ʔa lavohasi omiijaə, otahamaŋələɭao ʦaili.
aɭaʦəli ʔa lavohasi omiijaə, otahamaŋələɭao ʦaili.
S.TRANSL: My name is Lavohasi omiiyae, and I’m 10 years old.
What ethnic group are you from?

S.PHON: iɭaaə ʔa tali ɖəkai ʔoponoho ʔi ɭooɭai.
iɭaaə ʔa tali ɖəkai ʔoponoho ʔi ɭooɭai.

S.TRANSL: 你的家人都是原住民嗎?
Are all your family members indigenous?

S.FORM: iya'e, dha'ananaeli idhopelenai tali Drekai 'oponoho ocao.
S.PHON: ijaʔə, ðaʔananaəli iðopələnai tali ɖəkai ʔoponoho oʦao.

S.TRANSL: 是的，我們全家都是魯凱族人。
Yes, my whole family is Rukai.
你的家人都是原住民嗎?
Are all your family members indigenous?

S.FORM: 'aina, Amali 'a tali Drekai 'oponoho ocao, inali 'a Pakisa.
S.PHON: ʔaina, amali ʔa tali ɖəkai ʔoponoho oʦao, inali ʔa pakisa.

S.TRANSL: 不是，我的爸爸是魯凱族，我的媽媽是漢人。
No, my father is Rukai, and my mother is Han Taiwanese.
那位是你爸爸嗎? 他幾歲了?
Is that your father? How old is he?
那位是你爸爸嗎? 他幾歲了?
Is that your father? How old is he?

S.FORM: 'aina, ana 'a valriyonai ta kahodhangenga, otahamavalrolonga caili.

S.TRANSL: 不是，他是我們部落的耆老，他80歲了。
No, he is an elder of our tribe. He is 80 years old.

S.PHON: takali ʔaolai ʔa otahamaŋələŋa la kavaɭo ʦaili.
takali ʔaolai ʔa otahamaŋələŋa la kavaɭo ʦaili.

S.TRANSL: 你哥哥年紀比你大很多嗎?
Is your brother much older than you?
好久不見，你是不是長高了?
I haven’t seen you in a while. Have you grown taller?

S.FORM: apakacehemo'o, otahaeangalrao idhai la kamatolrolo kongfen.
S.PHON: apakaʦəhəmoʔo, otahaəaŋaɭao iðai la kamatoɭolo koŋ*ən.

S.TRANSL: I’m going to the office to see the teacher.
He’s going to throw away the trash.
他在游泳池，他等一下要比賽游泳。
He’s at the swimming pool. He’s going to compete in a swimming competition later.
Mooni在哪裡?她等一下要比賽跑步。
Where is Mooni? She’s going to compete in a running competition later.

S.PHON: omiki taʔasahovohovoə ðaʔanə, nao oiðə ʔaovaha.
omiki taʔasahovohovoə ðaʔanə, nao oiðə ʔaovaha.
S.TRANSL: She’s in the auditorium. I’ll go call her.
Let’s go to the playground together.

S.FORM: nao ivavaina moa longai kodhali, lamo oalraimiya'e 'ilape.
S.PHON: nao ivavaina moa loŋai koðali, lamo oaɭaimijaʔə ʔilapə.
S.FORM: nao ivavaina moa longai kodhali, lamo oalraimiya'e 'ilape.

S.TRANSL: 我先去買午餐，等一下去找你。
I’ll go buy lunch first, and then I’ll meet you later.
Let’s carry the lunch boxes together!

S.FORM: moa 'ilape dhoma ocao, amo oalrao 'ilape sinsi.
S.PHON: moa ʔilapə ðoma oʦao, amo oaɭao ʔilapə sinsi.
S.FORM: moa 'ilape dhoma ocao, amo oalrao 'ilape sinsi.
S.PHON: moa ʔilapə ðoma oʦao, amo oaɭao ʔilapə sinsi.
S.TRANSL: 你找別人，我要去找老師。
You find someone else, I’m going to find the teacher.

S.FORM: Vaaho mene amo pa'ikalromalromale kai kongokongo malri?
S.PHON: vaaho mənə amo paʔikaɭomaɭomalə kai koŋokoŋo maɭi?

S.PHON: vaaho mənə amo paʔikaɭomaɭomalə kai koŋokoŋo maɭi?
S.TRANSL: Is Vaaho competing in croquet today?
No, he’s competing in high jump.

S.FORM: tapakahakahalrae dhona menanae amo pakahakahai ka'o?
S.PHON: tapakahakahaɭaə ðona mənanaə amo pakahakahai kaʔo?

S.TRANSL: 運動會那天你會上場比賽嗎?
Will you compete in the sports day?

S.FORM: tapakahakahalrae dhona menanae amo pa'ikalromalromale ka'o?

S.TRANSL: 運動會那天你會上場比賽嗎?
Will you compete in the sports day?
我們運動會那天要做甚麼?
What will we do in the sports day?
We will compete in tug of war.
我們運動會要賣食物嗎?
Will we sell food at the sports day?
你獲得了第一名耶，真是厲害！
Congratulations on getting first place! You’re amazing!
Yes, she’s sleeping in her room.

S.FORM: omikikai ka, ta'asana molatadhe, amo 'oilri lo maongonga.
S.PHON: omikikai ka, taʔasana molataðə, amo ʔoiɭi lo maoŋoŋa.

S.TRANSL: 不在，她剛出去了，要晚上才回家。
No, she’s not. She just went out and will be back in the evening.

S.PHON: omiki ðaʔanə ɭahaɭə, moa ɭahaɭə ʔilapinə.
omiki ðaʔanə ɭahaɭə, moa ɭahaɭə ʔilapinə.
S.TRANSL: 他在二樓，你上去找他吧。
He’s upstairs. You can go up and find him.
是Vaaho阿，很久沒有看到你了，你好嗎?
It’s Vaaho. I haven’t seen you in a while! How have you been?

S.PHON: ʔakosaaəni ka mataaðiʔi! maavaʔiɭao nai ʔavaəvaəhə mimijaə.
ʔakosaaəni ka mataaðiʔi! maavaʔiɭao nai ʔavaəvaəhə mimijaə.
S.TRANSL: I’m doing great! I came to chat with you.

S.PHON: omiki tahahaʔə, hiŋihiŋi ʔaðamai, nao oiðə ʔaovaha.
omiki tahahaʔə, hiŋihiŋi ʔaðamai, nao oiðə ʔaovaha.
S.TRANSL: 他在廚房炒菜，我去叫他。
He’s in the kitchen cooking. Let me go call him.
She went to the chicken coop to catch chickens.

S.FORM: omonomi ka omikikai ka dha'ane, omiki ooma, 'odha'adhalridhe miki dha'ane.
S.PHON: omonomi ka omikikai ka ðaʔanə, omiki ooma, ʔoðaʔaðaɭiðə miki ðaʔanə.
S.FORM: omonomi ka omikikai ka dha'ane, omiki ooma, 'odha'adhalridhe miki dha'ane.
S.PHON: omonomi ka omikikai ka ðaʔanə, omiki ooma, ʔoðaʔaðaɭiðə miki ðaʔanə.
S.TRANSL: 你們的爺爺不在家，他在田裡，你們在家等他回來吧。
Your grandfather is not at home. He’s in the field. Wait for him to come back.
I haven’t seen you in a while. You’ve grown taller.
It’s so good to see you.

S.PHON: ʔina ʔa ʔavaʔailinomə ʔi paŋoðalə, opikimao piŋisjaŋə.
ʔina ʔa ʔavaʔailinomə ʔi paŋoðalə, opikimao piŋisjaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 這是給你們的鳳梨，要放到冰箱裡。
Here’s a pineapple for you. Put it in the refrigerator.

S.FORM: kani ii'aasadha kikaava'i, lanai 'ineyaelre kone aha'a omiyalrao.
S.PHON: kani iiʔaasaða kikaavaʔi, lanai ʔinəjaəɭə konə ahaʔa omijaɭao.
S.FORM: kani ii'aasadha kikaava'i, lanai 'ineyaelre kone aha'a omiyalrao.
S.PHON: kani iiʔaasaða kikaavaʔi, lanai ʔinəjaəɭə konə ahaʔa omijaɭao.
S.TRANSL: 這麼久沒來，我叫大家一起來吃飯。
It’s been a while. Let’s have a meal together.

S.PHON: kanikoaŋaʔo apatikoakoa, kama ʔoŋoloŋali ʔooʦija, taoaŋa ðələpə ʔavaəvaəhə.
kanikoaŋaʔo apatikoakoa, kama ʔoŋoloŋali ʔooʦija, taoaŋa ðələpə ʔavaəvaəhə.
S.TRANSL: 不用麻煩了，我喝茶就好，我們來客廳聊天。
No need to bother. I’ll just have tea. Let’s sit in the living room and chat.
謝謝你的招待，我要回去了。
Thank you for your hospitality. I have to go now.

S.FORM: 'ina tahokoko ta'asanalrao colo, omeca 'oilri ohamaca kone.
S.PHON: ʔina tahokoko taʔasanaɭao ʦolo, oməʦa ʔoiɭi ohamaʦa konə.
S.FORM: 'ina tahokoko ta'asanalrao colo, omeca 'oilri ohamaca kone.
S.PHON: ʔina tahokoko taʔasanaɭao ʦolo, oməʦa ʔoiɭi ohamaʦa konə.
S.TRANSL: 這隻雞剛殺好，你帶回去慢慢吃。
This chicken was just freshly killed. Take it home and enjoy it slowly.

S.PHON: amokoani ta paaləkatə lo ʔiða ʔaanə?
amokoani ta paaləkatə lo ʔiða ʔaanə?
S.TRANSL: 我和您約明天下午好嗎?
Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

S.FORM: nimokoani ta iki gako? amo loingimita.
S.PHON: nimokoani ta iki gako? amo loiŋimita.
S.FORM: nimokoani ta iki gako? amo loingimita.
S.PHON: nimokoani ta iki gako? amo loiŋimita.
S.TRANSL: 我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

S.FORM: ta oa ta'asahovohovoe mataadhiadhi'i, amo 'ikipingilrao kaocaae molraae.
S.PHON: ta oa taʔasahovohovoə mataaðiaðiʔi, amo ʔikipiŋiɭao kaoʦaaə moɭaaə.
S.FORM: ta oa ta'asahovohovoe mataadhiadhi'i, amo 'ikipingilrao kaocaae molraae.
S.PHON: ta oa taʔasahovohovoə mataaðiaðiʔi, amo ʔikipiŋiɭao kaoʦaaə moɭaaə.
S.TRANSL: 那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

S.FORM: valriyonai tapangale lo pongepangeni pa'apaalra. lo pongepangengai mava'imayae 'ilape!
S.PHON: vaɭijonai tapaŋalə lo poŋəpaŋəni paʔapaaɭa. lo poŋəpaŋəŋai mavaʔimajaə ʔilapə!
S.FORM: valriyonai tapangale lo pongepangeni pa'apaalra. lo pongepangengai mava'imayae 'ilape!
S.PHON: vaɭijonai tapaŋalə lo poŋəpaŋəni paʔapaaɭa. lo poŋəpaŋəŋai mavaʔimajaə ʔilapə!
S.TRANSL: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

S.FORM: lamo 'adha'adhao paokela lo kala vengelralrae, mo'adhaili tohamoho!
S.PHON: lamo ʔaðaʔaðao paokəla lo kala vəŋəɭaɭaə, moʔaðaili tohamoho!
S.FORM: lamo 'adha'adhao paokela lo kala vengelralrae, mo'adhaili tohamoho!
S.PHON: lamo ʔaðaʔaðao paokəla lo kala vəŋəɭaɭaə, moʔaðaili tohamoho!
S.TRANSL: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?
那你小米祭的時候來吧!
Then come during our Harvest Festival!
Alright! I’m looking forward to it!
請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?
I want to return to teach at the local school.
Why not teach in the city?

S.PHON: ʔiiðəðəməɭao ʔi vaɭijoli, ʔamaɭainə nao paviɭi omijaɭao vaɭijonai.
ʔiiðəðəməɭao ʔi vaɭijoli, ʔamaɭainə nao paviɭi omijaɭao vaɭijonai.
S.TRANSL: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

S.FORM: olriho'o mo'o dhohepe, aangai ta pato'oto'imiya'e?
S.PHON: oɭihoʔo moʔo ðohəpə, aaŋai ta patoʔotoʔimijaʔə?
S.FORM: olriho'o mo'o dhohepe, aangai ta pato'oto'imiya'e?
S.PHON: oɭihoʔo moʔo ðohəpə, aaŋai ta patoʔotoʔimijaʔə?
S.TRANSL: 你很會織布，是誰教你的?
You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

S.FORM: omoli 'avai ta pato'oto'iyae. omoli 'avai olriho'onga ta iki nai valriyo dhohepe.
S.PHON: omoli ʔavai ta patoʔotoʔijaə. omoli ʔavai oɭihoʔoŋa ta iki nai vaɭijo ðohəpə.
S.FORM: omoli 'avai ta pato'oto'iyae. omoli 'avai olriho'onga ta iki nai valriyo dhohepe.
S.PHON: omoli ʔavai ta patoʔotoʔijaə. omoli ʔavai oɭihoʔoŋa ta iki nai vaɭijo ðohəpə.
S.TRANSL: 是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.
How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

S.FORM: Ta dhahepe 'a bongkanai, nao pacongolo omiyalrao bongka.
S.PHON: ta ðahəpə ʔa boŋkanai, nao paʦoŋolo omijaɭao boŋka.
S.FORM: Ta dhahepe 'a bongkanai, nao pacongolo omiyalrao bongka.
S.PHON: ta ðahəpə ʔa boŋkanai, nao paʦoŋolo omijaɭao boŋka.
S.TRANSL: 織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

S.PHON: ʔina kavavaaə ʔakosaaənə ka mataaðiʔi ʔoŋolo.
ʔina kavavaaə ʔakosaaənə ka mataaðiʔi ʔoŋolo.

S.TRANSL: 你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

S.PHON: makapilikai, makapitoloni mənanaə moləkatə. oʦolokonamao piʔamaðalaə ðaʔakəʦaə vʦəŋə, mani ðaɭəsə poa paʔivo aɭəhəŋə voŋo ʦiʦijo, lamani poamao paʔano paloŋo.
S.FORM: makapilikai, makapitoloni menanae molekate. ocolokonamao pi'amadhalae dha'akecae vcenge, mani dhalrese poa pa'ivo alrehenge vongo ciciyo, lamani poamao pa'ano palongo.

S.TRANSL: 不用，大約一周就好了。把小米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.

S.FORM: 'ina omiki tavangolo la kahidhange, lredhekeli pooler 'ina!
S.PHON: ʔina omiki tavaŋolo la kahiðaŋə, ɭəðəkəli poolər ʔina!
S.FORM: 'ina omiki tavangolo la kahidhange, lredhekeli pooler 'ina!
S.PHON: ʔina omiki tavaŋolo la kahiðaŋə, ɭəðəkəli poolər ʔina!
S.TRANSL: 我這裡有辣椒和樹豆，都是我自己種的喔!
I have chili and pigeon pea, both grown by myself!

S.PHON: poəɭəɭə ʔa maðoʔa iðai la kamaɭimalə.
poəɭəɭə ʔa maðoʔa iðai la kamaɭimalə.

S.TRANSL: 我喜歡這件衣服，多少錢?
I like this piece of clothing. How much is it?
It’s too expensive. Can’t you lower the price a bit?

S.PHON: ðoɭokai, lo poʔaɭoɭoðoni amo ka ʔipaisokali.
ðoɭokai, lo poʔaɭoɭoðoni amo ka ʔipaisokali.
S.TRANSL: No, if I lower it further, I’ll lose money.

S.FORM: La pii'ana! nao alra matahea taecelrange kipingi.
S.PHON: la piiʔana! nao aɭa matahəa taəʦəɭaŋə kipiŋi.
S.FORM: La pii'ana! nao alra matahea taecelrange kipingi.
S.PHON: la piiʔana! nao aɭa matahəa taəʦəɭaŋə kipiŋi.
S.TRANSL: 好吧!那我要一件黑色的衣服。
Alright then, I’ll take a black piece of clothing.

S.PHON: amo aɭaɭao ʔina ʔaaloðo la ʔina paloŋo.
amo aɭaɭao ʔina ʔaaloðo la ʔina paloŋo.
S.TRANSL: 我要這把刀和這個鍋子。
I want this knife and this pot.

S.FORM: poelrelre 'a madho'a kodholo paiso, aangaali ta 'inipapi'a, amo to'ahakimo'o mo'adhaili.
S.PHON: poəɭəɭə ʔa maðoʔa koðolo paiso, aaŋaali ta ʔinipapiʔa, amo toʔahakimoʔo moʔaðaili.

S.TRANSL: 總共2000元，這是我自己手工打造的，保證可以用很久。
It’s a total of 2000 dollars. I handmade these, and they are guaranteed to last a long time.

S.FORM: noa pato'o ivokoli ladha kaava'i longai, aika'o kamo dhoolro tomelre'e?
S.PHON: noa patoʔo ivokoli laða kaavaʔi loŋai, aikaʔo kamo ðooɭo toməɭəʔə?
S.FORM: noa pato'o ivokoli ladha kaava'i longai, aika'o kamo dhoolro tomelre'e?
S.PHON: noa patoʔo ivokoli laða kaavaʔi loŋai, aikaʔo kamo ðooɭo toməɭəʔə?
S.TRANSL: 我介紹朋友來買，可以再便宜一些嗎?
I’ll introduce my friend to buy. Can you give a further discount?

S.FORM: la pii'ana! nao po'alrolrodhimiya'e toeaea kodholo la kaeneme idhai.
S.PHON: la piiʔana! nao poʔaɭoɭoðimijaʔə toəaəa koðolo la kaənəmə iðai.

S.PHON: la piiʔana! nao poʔaɭoɭoðimijaʔə toəaəa koðolo la kaənəmə iðai.
S.TRANSL: All right! It’s cheap for you, 1600yuan.

S.FORM: imiya oma cengele! olangalangai kipingi la tolropongo.
S.PHON: imija oma ʦəŋələ! olaŋalaŋai kipiŋi la toɭopoŋo.
S.FORM: imiya oma cengele! olangalangai kipingi la tolropongo.
S.PHON: imija oma ʦəŋələ! olaŋalaŋai kipiŋi la toɭopoŋo.
S.TRANSL: 來看看喔!衣服帽子都有賣。
Come and take a look! We sell clothes and hats.

S.FORM: kipingi ta kaeaea matolro idhai la kamalrimale paiso, ta katolro kipingi maeaea kodholo paiso.
S.PHON: kipiŋi ta kaəaəa matoɭo iðai la kamaɭimalə paiso, ta katoɭo kipiŋi maəaəa koðolo paiso.

S.TRANSL: One piece of clothing is 350 dollars, three pieces for 1000 dollars.

S.FORM: amo patahealrao longai, vo'alriyae ta kavaoa'e.
S.PHON: amo patahəaɭao loŋai, voʔaɭijaə ta kavaoaʔə.
S.FORM: amo patahealrao longai, vo'alriyae ta kavaoa'e.
S.PHON: amo patahəaɭao loŋai, voʔaɭijaə ta kavaoaʔə.
S.TRANSL: 我買一件就好，請給我新的。
I’ll just buy one piece, please give me a new one.

S.FORM: aika'o polringaao, nao langai omiyalrao valitoho va'oho la potavangolae alopae.
S.PHON: aikaʔo poɭiŋaao, nao laŋai omijaɭao valitoho vaʔoho la potavaŋolaə alopaə.

S.TRANSL: 你好，我想要買糯米飯和放辣椒的肉。
Hello, I would like to buy sticky rice and meat with chili peppers.

S.FORM: poelrengine longai va'oho, omikina ahingihingaye ping, amo langaika'o?
S.PHON: poəɭəŋinə loŋai vaʔoho, omikina ahiŋihiŋajə piŋ, amo laŋaikaʔo?
S.FORM: poelrengine longai va'oho, omikina ahingihingaye ping, amo langaika'o?
S.PHON: poəɭəŋinə loŋai vaʔoho, omikina ahiŋihiŋajə piŋ, amo laŋaikaʔo?
S.TRANSL: 飯賣完了。我還有蔥油餅，你要買嗎?
The rice is sold out. I have tana pancakes, would you like to buy them?

S.FORM: kanikoaadha! vo'alriyae pakaea vocongo, lo vilrilaenga lamo kavaa'ingalrao longai va'oho.
S.PHON: kanikoaaða! voʔaɭijaə pakaəa voʦoŋo, lo viɭilaəŋa lamo kavaaʔiŋaɭao loŋai vaʔoho.

S.TRANSL: 好吧!請給我一份，我下次再來買飯。
Alright, please give me one. I’ll come back next time to buy rice.

S.FORM: ya'e, nao pasopalriya'e potavisie, 'ina 'a mamalrimale paiso.
S.PHON: jaʔə, nao pasopaɭijaʔə potavisiə, ʔina ʔa mamaɭimalə paiso.
